title: CLI Usage Guide
bodyMarkdown:
Reference for the helios CLI, entry point at src/cli/main.ts.

## Commands
restore takes a manifest path and kicks off a full restore through the coordinator, printing the job id it can be tracked by. serve starts a local serving process on port 8080, useful for smoke testing without a full deploy. verify prints the resolved configuration without touching a cluster, the fastest way to confirm an env override actually took effect.

## Flags
--config points at a config file path, --prefetch-depth overrides HELIOS_PREFETCH_DEPTH for the invocation, --manifest is required for restore, --verbose enables debug-level logging. Flag parsing lives in src/cli/flags.ts and rejects unrecognized flags outright rather than silently ignoring them.

## Common mistakes
Forgetting --manifest on a restore call is the most common error, it fails fast with a clear message rather than doing nothing. Passing a non-integer to --prefetch-depth is rejected at parse time before any work starts.

## Extending the CLI
New commands go in the switch statement in main.ts, keep flag parsing changes isolated to flags.ts. Jonah Kim maintains this tooling, loop him in on anything beyond a small addition.
